item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My assumption regarding the ground is that individual grains of dirt are too small to fit a penny and don't count as a collective… my question is, what about stone? What would have happened if Larry had touched a mountain? Could Larry have gouged out huge chunks of the Earth's crust be accident, or do impure minerals not count?

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the original source couldn't be accessed with no indication the image was released under Creative Commons. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A terrible fate to have your skin turn into a copper currency. Not to mention having the 'Penny Midas' follow up by causing your brain to collapse.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Bill Sorkin was named D-1015-01 following this interview."

here's my +1

item.title: Safe? SAFE?
item.content:encoded:
Research originally suggested that pennies created by SCP-1015 had no anomalous properties. It is now known that at an as-of-yet unidentified percentage of pennies created this way exhibit the anomalous effects of SCP-1015-1.

Further instances are to be contained

So there is an unknown amount - easily thousands, if not millions - of uncontained SCP-1015-1 instances capable of parasitic replication? Not to mention the likely impossible task of tracking all of them down?

If the SCP file could speak, it would be screaming "KETER".

Otherwise great article, +1.

item.title: Staff Post - Reversion Note (Closed)
item.content:encoded: Noting that a recent edit by Chase Dale has been reverted. Non-authors should not make content changes in articles they did not write.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Big fan of this entry. Really well-paced and just the right amount of content.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: EDIT: posted this as a reply by accident. delete if possible, cheers

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If I never see another penny again, it’ll be too soon.

became agitated, demanding he be released, reunited with his brother (Larry Sorkin, whom subject was unaware was killed during item recovery), and allowed to keep "our penny"

hm.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In my headcanon there are several subtypes of D-class personnel. Mass murderer, rapist, terrorist? To most dangerous Keters. Random hobo or some loser picked up from refugee camp? Go eat that endlessly multiplying cake.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
that poor homeless man became a D-Class
I mean , He is just a Homeless man , not a rapist or murderer.

An individual doesn't need to be a rapist or a murderer to be a D-Class. Technically they'd just need to be someone in good health who can follow orders, whom the public won't notice missing.

Furthermore, not all SCPs are dangerous or terrifying. D-Class personnel can be assigned to something like SCP-2255, which inhabits a Foundation-maintained tropical island.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The only problem I have with this is the fact that poor homeless man became a D-Class
I mean , He is just a Homeless man , not a rapist or murderer.
other than that , I Enjoyed that lolfoundation a bit more than I should have
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thinking in the same vein as Doctor Xinc, it seem like you can use this to neutralize some self-propagating SCPs; you can touch some of those multiplying cakes and see if their effects are stopped, for example

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ok. I really liked this SCP, I was just a little confused by that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can't believe I randomly stopped by within a week of the first comment on my first SCP in seven months. I can answer a question without necroposting!

In short, I imagine he wrapped it in a dish towel or something. Or maybe he wore rubber gloves.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If everything the guy in the last incident log touches turns to pennies then how come he can shoot himself? Wouldn't the gun just turn into pennies as soon as he picked it up?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I disagree, on the grounds that it's funny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Test 1015-05
Subject: █████ (11.5 hours after exposure to SCP-1015-1), D-1015-01
Test: D-1015-01 became agitated, demanding he be released, reunited with his brother (Larry Sorkin, whom subject was unaware was killed during item recovery), and allowed to keep “our penny.” Quickly attacked █████, fracturing █████’s nose, right orbital bone and right cheekbone, and knocking several teeth loose, rendering him unconscious. D-1015-01 then retrieved SCP-1015-1 from █████'s hand.
Results: SCP-1015-1 remained in D-1015-01’s possession. █████ ceased causing transmutation.
Note: That wasn’t a test! He just beat the ████ out of me while you recorded it!
Note: Welcome back, Trevor. Sorry about the face.

This is lolfoundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fixed a little error (Larry was referred to as Bill at one point, heh).

Overall, great read. Like others, I am a little iffy with ol' Bill becoming a D-Class, but it didn't bother me too much. The thought of a man mostly being transformed into pennies but not all of him is just about enough for me to upvote in itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mostly? This is still a writing site and these are still stories. The interest of hard-to-kill SCPs are partially in the puzzle of killing them. Keeping the puzzle open ended helps keep the puzzle still new and interesting to others. "And then Able was killed forever by this other scp" leaves a boring ending to an interesting story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why not use SCP-this on SCP-076-1? I think it'd be an effective weapon against Hard-to-kill SCPs (ones that the foundation wants to destroy) would it not be an effective weapon? Also, could one wear thin and tight rubber gloves to prevent transmutation?
This article deserves it more than most. Excellent work LaserGoose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shouldn't the have the "ectoentropic" tag, since it generates mass when transforming anything less dense than copper?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The numbering of the Addenda seems to be messed up: 1015-1, 1015-01, 1015-02.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ooh wow. A seemingly whimsical object with a very sinister story written around it and some body horror added in for good measure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, they're homeless, so he probably wasn't drinking from a glass, maybe a styrofoam cup, which is probably thicker than a penny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this one, but have a few issues with it.

First, you say he has to drink out of a straw, but later say that glasses and liquids are unaffected by the ability.

Second, you say that Bill had to cut up Larry's food, but you also say that objects that aren't the size of a penny in any direction won't be transmuted. A fork is not as wide as a penny at the handle, at least most aren't. So how come he couldn't hold one? In the same vein, it seems like not all of the guy's skin should have been converted, since there are areas (like fingers) where skin isn't as wide as a penny, and some areas where the skin is curved so a penny couldn't fit, like the glass test.

Third

(Please let’s never do this again.)

Which I shouldn't have to explain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To each their own of course, but I'm not sure how even a death row D-class would be anything other than "work for us or we'll shoot you." It's not a pretty concept no matter how you look at it….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice! Two things that bug me are:
- in my headcanon people do not become D-class because they were in the wrong place at the wrong time; such people get dosed with amnesiacs and released. There is a reason for why the D-class are recruited from among inmates and it is not that the Foundation would have any qualms about using "innocent civilians" as test fodder. It it simply that being offered what they perceive an improvement of their situation motivates them to cooperate (and between lethal tests and monthly terminations, they haven't got time to catch on). There is no real point in using people whose only motivation to work for you is "or we will shoot you";
- I am pretty sure the Foundation wouldn't have used the surviving Sorkin brother as a subject in these tests unless a good reason existed to use someone already familiar with the properties of the tested object, as it could potentially skew test results. In this particular case it probably doesn't make a difference - but even so.

That said, I have upvoted the article anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's expunged for a reason, most likely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Find a penny, pick it up, for the rest of your life you're pretty F*.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It just hit me that there are probably only a pair of these.

Someone threw in their two cents.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Which end did the penny come out of after they removed his clothes?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What really makes this for me is the "FOR SCIENCE!" in the logs. Relentless, systematic examination of the object's properties, presented in a way that serves as a creepy narrative.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No, it is Safe. It meets the locked box test-if you put in a locked box, nothing happens.

I like this one. A little part of me would like to find the one that makes quarters, and the rest of me thinks that part is crazy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Solid work, only thing that bears correcting in my opinion is reclassification from Safe to Euclid due to its self-propagating nature.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I admit I really had to skim the testing logs, and in fact am of the opinion that the article could've done without them.

However, the main body of the article and the first interview is a really interesting read. And since I was part of the critique group for this, I'm glad it turned out so well. And…

It’s … God, it’s so much worse than you’d think. Because your body … it’s not all one piece. There are lots of parts. So many little pieces…

Definitely my favorite part, and clinched the upvote right there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for putting a use to the most useless coin.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, we found out how to talk to an alchemist finally!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The goose speaks the truth, many of the D-class are likely just people who were in the wrong place at the wrong time. People who cannot be released back into the Unsuspecting World without there being problems or death. Some people are simply victims of some other-worldly tragedy, who then become 'victims' of the Foundation in order to protect the rest of humanity. Look at SCP-231 for an example

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Also, SCP-1015, not SCP-1015-1.

In what context?

Either Bill was a mass murderering serial killer on the side, or the doctor in charge was they guy who fed kids to 682.

I'll just say this once more, then let the topic go unless you think it's worth revisiting. I refer you to the site FAQ. Read below where it says "So, I want to write an SCP or story, what canon do I have to keep in mind?"

tl;dr: it's possible not all D-class deserve to be D-class. I can understand you not liking the foundation being evil for the sake of evil, though. Neither do I. I just don't see this in that way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …except there's no indication of violence before his brother was shot to death by pennies, and there are amnesiacs…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
He's a violent alcoholic drifter with no family, he knows too much, and as far as anyone else knows he's already dead.

Plus we do need to know if SCP-1015 can be deactivated by killing the owner (answer: yes).

Ethics Committee ruling: regrettable but acceptable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm just going to reiterate what LosER said:

I never have liked the idea of the Foundation being heartless, but the whole "D-classes don't grow on trees, at least until we get O5 approval for that project" principle influenced me here. You've got a drifter with presumably no ties, who might as well have died in that fire, and he knows about an item you're trying to contain, and he's gullible. Solid D-class material there. If you're heartless.

It's not so much evil as "I sincerely don't care about people, and this is rather convenient." And yes, there is a difference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
K, Downvoted.

Closing Statement: Bill Sorkin was named D-1015-01 following this interview

Either Bill was a mass murderering serial killer on the side, or the doctor in charge was they guy who fed kids to 682. Then there's one of the logs halfway into it. It's a cariature of quite a few other articles.

Also, SCP-1015, not SCP-1015-1.

The imagry is good, but this is over the top "LOL evul foundayshun!!!" for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Makes sense to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Most (if not all) of those pieces are contiguous, though. As parts of the whole, one would think them exempt.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 1015-2 is the owner of the penny. Because the penny does nothing without an owner, it seemed prudent to me to make the owner a component of the item. Maybe not. Anyone else think it's unnecessarily confusing?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is this always referred to as 1015-1? It just seems to make it more confusing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Key phrase at the end there.
The first draft had MC&D trying this, which may have been feasible for them, if not quite as profitable as their usual fare. But after talking to some folks in chat, it seems more likely, to me at least (and more fun to write) that a couple of ne'er-do-wells would try this scheme. Had they lived long enough, I'm sure they'd have realized it'd be better to just save enough to rent a plow, then go transmute a couple parking lots.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I never have liked the idea of the Foundation being heartless, but the whole "D-classes don't grow on trees, at least until we get O5 approval for that project" principle influenced me here. You've got a drifter with presumably no ties, who might as well have died in that fire, and he knows about an item you're trying to contain, and he's gullible. Solid D-class material there. If you're heartless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Smelting enough copper down to make it worth while is not a minor operation. These are guys getting drunk on '40. Seriously - change a few rocks and you've got the coin to get drunk again. Something like a gallon of pennies = $30 by value (not copper).

But, to be honest, they don't seem like the brightest bulbs in the drawer….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: More than that. Copper's a valuable commodity lately. So much so that in a lot of places burglars are breaking into homes specifically to steal the wiring.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pennies from 1974 were almost completely copper, and worth about 2 cents a piece. If they used Coinstar, they'd only get 1 cent per penny, and then the Coinstar fee would subtracted from the profit. Melting the pennies down to sell as copper would make double the cash.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But there's never enough rapists to go around…

Or the Foundation makes random hobos commit rape through mind control and turns them into D-class.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Melting them down? Seriously?

Have they never seen a Coinstar machine?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great read. Good work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But, I'm not a fan of Billy becoming a D-Class. That sort of thing is reserved for murderers, rapists, pedophiles, etc.
Not homeless men who get caught up in a penny-forging scheme.

I'd rather see him get Amnisiac'd instead.

EDIT: I mean, I upvote it either way, but still.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
… God, it’s so much worse than you’d think. Because your body … it’s not all one piece. There are lots of parts. So many little pieces…

yeah, upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am very glad to replaced the MC&D stuff with this, Goosey. Upvoted no problem.
